pull_request.title: Drop Python 2.7 support
pull_request.body:
Python 2.7 has been EOL since January 2020. This removes compatibility shims, conditional imports, and version guards that existed solely to support Python 2.

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Drops Python 2.7 support and sets the minimum to Python 3.6. Removes compatibility shims, simplifies I/O and Tkinter code, and updates docs, packaging, and tests.

- **Refactors**
  - Removed __future__ imports, raw_input/input shims, and version guards across utilities.
  - Simplified Tkinter usage in guiconfig.py; use tkinter.*, iconphoto(True, …), and Text.replace().
  - Standardized open(..., encoding="utf-8") and removed universal newline hacks.
  - Deleted _IS_PY2 and Python 2 branches in kconfiglib; always use dict.values(), re.ASCII, and decode subprocess output.
  - Cleaned menuconfig and examples (no print_function, unified erasechar decoding).
  - Updated README/KBUILD to “Python 3.6+”, pruned Python 2 instructions.
  - setup.py: python_requires=">=3.6", removed Python 2 classifiers; setup.cfg no universal wheel.
  - tests: reltest runs only python3; testsuite unconditionally runs warn-undef checks.

- **Migration**
  - Use Python 3.6+ for all tools (menuconfig.py, guiconfig.py, scripts).
  - Update scripts/CI to invoke python3 instead of python2.
  - On Windows, install windows-curses for menuconfig; ensure Tkinter (python3-tk) is installed where needed.

<sup>Written for commit b6a23cef11a1c8d5e3da539a477f558e94f9ba3f. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

changed_files.patch:
@@ -107,7 +107,7 @@ Functions are implemented in `kconfiglib.py` following these principles:
 
 - Uniform interface through the `_functions` dictionary
 - No special-case handling
-- Python 2.7+ and 3.2+ compatibility using standard library only
+- Python 3.6+ using standard library only
 - Graceful error handling (missing tools return `n`)
 
 ### Environment Variables

@@ -68,8 +68,7 @@ such as unused symbols, symbols that can never be enabled, pointless menuconfig
 prefixes in source code. Run it directly with `python3 lint.py <Kconfig>` or see `python3 lint.py --help`
 for available checks.
 
-Starting with Kconfiglib version 12.2.0, all utilities are compatible with both Python 2 and Python 3.
-Previously, `menuconfig.py` was limited to Python 3, so it now offers improved backward compatibility.
+Kconfiglib requires Python 3.6 or later.
 
 **Note:** If Kconfiglib is installed with the `pip --user` flag,
 ensure that the `PATH` variable includes the directory where the executables are installed.
@@ -95,14 +94,10 @@ is installed.
 
 See the module docstring at the top of [kconfiglib.py](kconfiglib.py).
 
-### Python version compatibility (2.7/3.2+)
+### Python version compatibility
 
-Kconfiglib and all of its utilities run under Python 2.7 as well as Python 3.2 and later.
-The code primarily relies on basic Python features and does not depend on third-party libraries,
-making it relatively easy to maintain backward compatibility.
-
-The Python 3.2 requirement stems from using the `argparse` module and the `format()` function with unnumbered braces (`{}`).
-If given a choice, using a more recent Python 3 release is recommended due to its improved Unicode handling.
+Kconfiglib requires Python 3.6 or later.
+The code primarily relies on basic Python features and does not depend on third-party libraries.
 
 ## Getting started
 
@@ -339,7 +334,7 @@ This warning can also be toggled by setting `Kconfig.warn_assign_undef` to `True
 
 - Windows support
 
-  Nothing in Kconfiglib is specific to Linux. Universal newline mode is used for both Python 2 and Python 3.
+  Nothing in Kconfiglib is specific to Linux.
   The [Zephyr](https://www.zephyrproject.org/) project uses Kconfiglib to generate `.config` files and C headers on both Linux and Windows.
 
 - Internals that (mostly) mirror the C implementation
@@ -358,9 +353,7 @@ Three configuration interfaces are currently available:
 
   There is also a show-help mode that displays the help text of the currently selected symbol in the bottom help window.
 
-  Starting with Kconfiglib 12.2.0, `menuconfig.py` runs under both Python 2 and Python 3
-  (it previously ran only under Python 3, so this was a backport).
-  Running it under Python 3 provides better support for Unicode text entry (`get_wch()` is unavailable in the Python 2 `curses` module).
+  `menuconfig.py` requires Python 3.6+.
 
   There are no third-party dependencies on Unix-like systems.
   On Windows, the `curses` module is not included by default, but can be added by installing the `windows-curses` package:
@@ -384,18 +377,16 @@ Three configuration interfaces are currently available:
   Toggle between themes via the Theme menu.
   The interface includes a responsive layout that adapts to window resizing.
 
-  `guiconfig.py` has been tested on X11, Windows, and macOS, and it works with both Python 2 and Python 3.
+  `guiconfig.py` has been tested on X11, Windows, and macOS, and requires Python 3.6+.
 
   Although Tkinter is part of the Python standard library, it is not always installed by default on Linux.
   The commands below install it on a few different systems:
   - Ubuntu/Debian:
     ```shell
-    sudo apt install python-tk
     sudo apt install python3-tk
     ```
   - Fedora:
     ```shell
-    dnf install python2-tkinter
     dnf install python3-tkinter
     ```
   - Arch:
@@ -496,7 +487,7 @@ The test suite consists of a set of self-tests and a set of compatibility tests
 generated by Kconfiglib with those generated by the C tools across various scenarios.
 See [testsuite.py](testsuite.py) for available options.
 
-The [tests/reltest](tests/reltest) script runs the test suite and all example scripts under both Python 2 and Python 3 to verify that everything works as expected.
+The [tests/reltest](tests/reltest) script runs the test suite and all example scripts to verify that everything works as expected.
 
 Occasionally, the C tools' output may change slightly (for example, due to a [recent change](https://www.spinics.net/lists/linux-kbuild/msg17074.html)).
 If the test suite reports failures, try running it again against the [linux-next tree](https://www.kernel.org/doc/man-pages/linux-next.html),

-        #
-        # - Enable universal newlines mode on Python 2 to ease
-        #   interoperability between Linux and Windows. It's already the

-        #
-        #   The "U" flag would currently work for both Python 2 and 3, but it's
-        #   deprecated on Python 3, so play it future-safe.
-        #
-        #   io.open() defaults to universal newlines on Python 2 (and is an
-        #   alias for open() on Python 3), but it returns 'unicode' strings and

-        #       user  0m1.040s

-        #   There's no appreciable performance difference between "r" and
-        #   "rU" for parsing performance on Python 2.

-        #   turns strings into Unicode strings, which gets messy. Python 2
-        #   doesn't decode regular strings anyway.

 warning: the int symbol INT (defined at Kconfiglib/tests/Kundef:8) has a non-int range [UNDEF_2 (undefined), 8 (undefined)]

     shutil.rmtree(tmpdir)
 
-    # Python 2/3 compatible
     for key, sym in kconf.syms.items():
         verify(isinstance(key, str), "weird key '{}' in syms dict".format(key))

commit_summaries.message:
Drop Python 2.7 support

Python 2.7 has been EOL since January 2020. This removes compatibility
shims, conditional imports, and version guards that existed solely to
support Python 2.
